sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The initial state from history[0] shows very different items:
- Y: 23px, boundary=[[28, 9], [28, 11], [35, 11], [35, 9]] - this is a vertical bar (7 rows x 3 cols)
- N: 14px, boundary=[[9, 28], [9, 32], [11, 32], [11, 28]] - this is a horizontal bar (3 rows x 4 cols)
But the current state has:
- Y: 42px, boundary=[[27, 25], [27, 38], [29, 38], [29, 25]] - horizontal bar (3 rows x 14 cols)
- N: 78px, boundary=[[28, 39], [28, 41], [53, 41], [53, 39]] - vertical bar (26 rows x 3 cols)
- O: 42px, boundary=[[39, 10], [39, 23], [41, 23], [41, 10]] - horizontal bar (3 rows x 14 cols)
- S: 33px, boundary=[[30, 24], [30, 26], [40, 26], [40, 24]] - vertical bar (11 rows x 3 cols)
So the initial state only had Y and N items, and they were much smaller. The O and S items appeared later (after I grew them).
This suggests that the items start small and grow. The goal might be to grow them to match the L-shape.
But wait, the initial state only had Y and N. Where did O and S come from? They must have appeared when I grew them.
Actually, looking at the initial state, Y was a vertical bar (7 rows x 3 cols) and N was a horizontal bar (3 rows x 4 cols). But in the current state, Y is a horizontal bar and N is a vertical bar. So the items changed orientation!
This is very strange. Let me think about what happened.
Actually, I think the initial state from history[0] might not be the true initial state of the level. It might be the state after the first action. Let me check the transitions to understand the sequence of actions.
Actually, let me focus on the current state and figure out what to do next. The items are at:
- Y: 42px, horizontal (3 rows x 14 cols)
- O: 42px, horizontal (3 rows x 14 cols)
- S: 33px, vertical (11 rows x 3 cols)
- N: 78px, vertical (26 rows x 3 cols)
The L-shape has:
- Horizontal bar: 72px (3 rows x 24 cols)
- Left vertical: 45px (15 rows x 3 cols)
- Right vertical: 90px (30 rows x 3 cols)
The items need to match the L-shape. The horizontal bar is 24 cols, and Y + O = 14 + 14 = 28 cols. So one of them needs to shrink by 4 cols.
The left vertical is 15 rows, and S is 11 rows. So S needs to grow by 4 rows.
The right vertical is 30 rows, and N is 26 rows. So N needs to grow by 4 rows.
But I've tried growing S and N, and they were no-impact (already at max). And I've tried shrinking Y and O, and they worked.
Wait, let me re-examine. When I grew S (blue), it was no-impact. When I grew N (green), it went from 30px to 78px. So N can grow, but S can't.
Let me try shrinking Y and O to see if they can reach the target sizes.
Actually, I realize I need to reconsider the game mechanics. The items form a connected staircase shape, and growing/shrinking one item affects the others. The goal might not be to match the L-shape's dimensions exactly, but to make the items overlap with the L-shape in a specific way.
Let me try a different approach. Instead of trying to match dimensions, let me try to make the items overlap with the L-shape by moving them.
The L-shape is at rows 9-44, cols 33-56. The items are at rows 27-53, cols 10-41. The items are below and to the left of the L-shape.
To make the items overlap with the L-shape, I need to move them up and to the right. Growing orange moves them right, and shrinking orange moves them left. What about moving them up/down?
Let me try growing/shrinking yellow (Y) to see if it moves the items up/down.
